title: Chrysanthemum Tea

seo.title: How to Make Chrysanthemum Tea — Easy Chinese Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Chrysanthemum Tea. This easy Chinese recipe includes step-by-step instructions, tips, and serving ideas.
h2Headings: Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageCaption: Delicious homemade Chrysanthemum Tea — a beloved Chinese classic

description: Chrysanthemum Tea is a fragrant and refreshing herbal infusion, cherished in Chinese culture for its delicate floral aroma and cooling properties. Made from dried chrysanthemum flowers, this golden brew is naturally caffeine-free and often enjoyed for its purported health benefits. It is a perfect beverage to soothe and relax, served either hot or chilled.

instructions.title: Prepare the Chrysanthemum Flowers
instructions.text: Measure out the dried chrysanthemum flowers. Ensure they are food-grade and free from impurities.

instructions.title: Rinse the Flowers
instructions.text: Place the dried chrysanthemum flowers in a small strainer and quickly rinse them under cool running water. This helps to remove any dust or debris. Drain well.

instructions.title: Boil Water
instructions.text: In a teapot or saucepan, bring 4 cups of filtered water to a rolling boil.

instructions.title: Add Flowers and Steep
instructions.text: Once the water is boiling, carefully add the rinsed chrysanthemum flowers to the hot water. Remove the pot from the heat or turn off the teapot.

instructions.title: Steep the Tea
instructions.text: Cover the teapot or saucepan with a lid and let the chrysanthemum flowers steep for 7-10 minutes, depending on desired strength. The water will turn a beautiful golden yellow color.

instructions.title: Sweeten and Serve
instructions.text: After steeping, strain the tea into serving cups. If desired, add rock sugar or honey to taste and stir until dissolved. Serve hot immediately or chill for later.

tips: For a stronger flavor, you can gently press the steeped flowers with the back of a spoon before straining.
Always use high-quality, food-grade dried chrysanthemum flowers for the best flavor and safety. Different varieties offer slightly different aromas.
Chrysanthemum tea can be stored in the refrigerator for up to 2-3 days. It's delightful served over ice with a slice of lemon during warmer weather.
article: Chrysanthemum Tea is a beloved Chinese Beverages dish enjoyed across Chinese. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
